violations.description: File exceeds TEA DoD target (<300 lines), increasing cognitive load and debugging cost.
violations.suggestion: Split by AC into focused files (tokens/primitives, display-safety, responsive behavior).

violations.description: URL builder and context helpers are duplicated across ATDD and automate E2E specs.
violations.suggestion: Extract shared g-1 URL/test utilities under tests/support/helpers/connectshyft-g1.ts

violations.description: Automate spec repeats route builders and css reader helpers from ATDD spec.
violations.suggestion: Reuse shared helper module with typed exports for URL and metric readers.

violations.description: Tests only encode priority labels; structured story-level test IDs are missing.
violations.suggestion: Adopt stable IDs like G1-ATDD-API-001 in titles for traceability.

violations.description: Nested fallback branches increase complexity and reduce readability in core scenarios.
violations.suggestion: Normalize fixture preconditions and split alternate paths into separate tests.

violations.description: Inline object-walking utility logic is dense and repeated for payload extraction.
violations.suggestion: Extract reusable projection helper with explicit typing and unit tests.

recommendations: Split oversized E2E ATDD file into AC-scoped specs
Deduplicate g-1 helper logic across ATDD and automate suites
Adopt structured test IDs for story-level traceability
summary: Maintainability is the weakest dimension due to oversize file scope, duplicated helpers, and branch-heavy scenarios.
